programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.title: Fujian’s Tulou – The Mysterious Earthen Castles
programme.desc: The tulous, or earth-built dwellings, of Fujian are recognized as treasures of traditional architecture, thanks to their unique design, cultural importance, and embodiment of the concept of harmonious coexistence with nature.\nTulous are mostly rectangular or circular in form, with a simple, elegant exterior and a well-furnished interior. As homes built by the Hakka people, they are imbued with diligence, wisdom, and the pursuit of excellence. Surrounded by mountains and waters, the tulous blend well with the natural environment. When building their tulous, the Hakka people pay attention to ecological balance in order to minimize their environmental footprint.\nA tulou is not only a safe place to live, but also the crystallization of the culture and architectural skill passed down for generations among the Hakka people.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 1
programme.desc: Nanjing, an ancient city that served as the capital of six imperial dynasties, stands as a testament to China's long-standing history and rich culture. From its Porcelain Tower - one of the Seven Wonders of the Middle Ages, to its grand city walls and ancient palaces, join us on a voyage across time to experience the echoes of history in Nanjing's storied bricks.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: The Rite Society
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 2
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the grand old mansions of Nanjing’s merchant families and take a stroll through their exquisite gardens, where Nature is incorporated into the architecture. From centuries-old Qing Dynasty residences to traditional folk handicrafts, join us on a deep dive into Nanjing’s rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Basketball dreams soar in Liangshan
programme.desc: Meet basketball girl Aguolieha, who comes from a rural village in the mountainous Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture in southwest China. Playing for a local team called the Blackhawks, the 13-year-old has a big dream: to become a professional player one day and join Team China. At first, her father was not supportive as he feared that sports training would make her grades fall. Proving to be excellent both academically and on the court, she often looks up to her idol Stephen Curry, and trains hard with her peers. She believes that the sky's the limit and that basketball games will help her dreams soar.

programme.title: The sound of casting
programme.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programme.desc:
The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.
The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.
Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.
Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and funerals, the Grand Song, as a valuable part of the Dong ethnic group’s cultural heritage, reflects their wisdom and creativity, as well as their harmony with nature.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.title: Fujian’s Tulou – The Mysterious Earthen Castles
programme.desc: The tulous, or earth-built dwellings, of Fujian are recognized as treasures of traditional architecture, thanks to their unique design, cultural importance, and embodiment of the concept of harmonious coexistence with nature.\nTulous are mostly rectangular or circular in form, with a simple, elegant exterior and a well-furnished interior. As homes built by the Hakka people, they are imbued with diligence, wisdom, and the pursuit of excellence. Surrounded by mountains and waters, the tulous blend well with the natural environment. When building their tulous, the Hakka people pay attention to ecological balance in order to minimize their environmental footprint.\nA tulou is not only a safe place to live, but also the crystallization of the culture and architectural skill passed down for generations among the Hakka people.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 1
programme.desc: Nanjing, an ancient city that served as the capital of six imperial dynasties, stands as a testament to China's long-standing history and rich culture. From its Porcelain Tower - one of the Seven Wonders of the Middle Ages, to its grand city walls and ancient palaces, join us on a voyage across time to experience the echoes of history in Nanjing's storied bricks.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: The Rite Society
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 2
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the grand old mansions of Nanjing’s merchant families and take a stroll through their exquisite gardens, where Nature is incorporated into the architecture. From centuries-old Qing Dynasty residences to traditional folk handicrafts, join us on a deep dive into Nanjing’s rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Basketball dreams soar in Liangshan
programme.desc: Meet basketball girl Aguolieha, who comes from a rural village in the mountainous Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture in southwest China. Playing for a local team called the Blackhawks, the 13-year-old has a big dream: to become a professional player one day and join Team China. At first, her father was not supportive as he feared that sports training would make her grades fall. Proving to be excellent both academically and on the court, she often looks up to her idol Stephen Curry, and trains hard with her peers. She believes that the sky's the limit and that basketball games will help her dreams soar.

programme.title: The sound of casting
programme.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programme.desc:
The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.
The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.
Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.
Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and funerals, the Grand Song, as a valuable part of the Dong ethnic group’s cultural heritage, reflects their wisdom and creativity, as well as their harmony with nature.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.title: Fujian’s Tulou – The Mysterious Earthen Castles
programme.desc: The tulous, or earth-built dwellings, of Fujian are recognized as treasures of traditional architecture, thanks to their unique design, cultural importance, and embodiment of the concept of harmonious coexistence with nature.\nTulous are mostly rectangular or circular in form, with a simple, elegant exterior and a well-furnished interior. As homes built by the Hakka people, they are imbued with diligence, wisdom, and the pursuit of excellence. Surrounded by mountains and waters, the tulous blend well with the natural environment. When building their tulous, the Hakka people pay attention to ecological balance in order to minimize their environmental footprint.\nA tulou is not only a safe place to live, but also the crystallization of the culture and architectural skill passed down for generations among the Hakka people.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: Inseparability of Things
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Dreams beyond the acrobatic stage
programme.desc: Many young acrobats from across China have gathered in Wuqiao, Cangzhou in north China's Hebei Province, with one shared goal: to dazzle audiences with their talents and stunt skills. They usually starts to train at an early age, shouldering the hopes and support of their families and teachers to become successful performers. We followed three of these young performers to show you how incredibly talented they are.

programme.title: Echoes of Green
programme.desc: Echoes of Green takes viewers deep into Anji’s bamboo forests and mountain villages, where a new generation of Chinese youth is redefining how to live with nature. Through stories of outdoor sports, forest cafés, tea rituals, and family farms, the series shows how ecological ideals have become everyday choices and creative lifestyles. It is not just about landscapes, but about people — young dreamers proving that prosperity and nature can grow side by side, and that culture gives life to sustainability.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the \"king of brocades.\" It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as \"weaving through the warp and cutting the weft\"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: A Vibe of the Village: The Beat of Xiangxi Drums
programme.desc: Experience the vibrant power of Xiangxi Drum Dance. This program delves into the heart of Tujia and Miao cultures, showcasing the rhythmic beats and spirited performances that have echoed through West Hunan's mountains for centuries. Discover the stories, traditions, and raw energy of this captivating folk art.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: Inseparability of Things
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Dreams beyond the acrobatic stage
programme.desc: Many young acrobats from across China have gathered in Wuqiao, Cangzhou in north China's Hebei Province, with one shared goal: to dazzle audiences with their talents and stunt skills. They usually starts to train at an early age, shouldering the hopes and support of their families and teachers to become successful performers. We followed three of these young performers to show you how incredibly talented they are.

programme.title: Echoes of Green
programme.desc: Echoes of Green takes viewers deep into Anji’s bamboo forests and mountain villages, where a new generation of Chinese youth is redefining how to live with nature. Through stories of outdoor sports, forest cafés, tea rituals, and family farms, the series shows how ecological ideals have become everyday choices and creative lifestyles. It is not just about landscapes, but about people — young dreamers proving that prosperity and nature can grow side by side, and that culture gives life to sustainability.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the \"king of brocades.\" It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as \"weaving through the warp and cutting the weft\"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: A Vibe of the Village: The Beat of Xiangxi Drums
programme.desc: Experience the vibrant power of Xiangxi Drum Dance. This program delves into the heart of Tujia and Miao cultures, showcasing the rhythmic beats and spirited performances that have echoed through West Hunan's mountains for centuries. Discover the stories, traditions, and raw energy of this captivating folk art.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: Inseparability of Things
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Dreams beyond the acrobatic stage
programme.desc: Many young acrobats from across China have gathered in Wuqiao, Cangzhou in north China's Hebei Province, with one shared goal: to dazzle audiences with their talents and stunt skills. They usually starts to train at an early age, shouldering the hopes and support of their families and teachers to become successful performers. We followed three of these young performers to show you how incredibly talented they are.

programme.title: Echoes of Green
programme.desc: Echoes of Green takes viewers deep into Anji’s bamboo forests and mountain villages, where a new generation of Chinese youth is redefining how to live with nature. Through stories of outdoor sports, forest cafés, tea rituals, and family farms, the series shows how ecological ideals have become everyday choices and creative lifestyles. It is not just about landscapes, but about people — young dreamers proving that prosperity and nature can grow side by side, and that culture gives life to sustainability.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the \"king of brocades.\" It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as \"weaving through the warp and cutting the weft\"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: A Vibe of the Village: The Beat of Xiangxi Drums
programme.desc: Experience the vibrant power of Xiangxi Drum Dance. This program delves into the heart of Tujia and Miao cultures, showcasing the rhythmic beats and spirited performances that have echoed through West Hunan's mountains for centuries. Discover the stories, traditions, and raw energy of this captivating folk art.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: The Art of Harmony: Inseparability of Things
programme.desc: “The Art of Harmony” is a documentary about Confucianism, produced by CGTN and Discovery Channel. It highlights the timeless nature of the sage’s philosophy and its global relevance today. Join us in exploring how the world might benefit from the pursuit of “The Art of Harmony”.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Dreams beyond the acrobatic stage
programme.desc: Many young acrobats from across China have gathered in Wuqiao, Cangzhou in north China's Hebei Province, with one shared goal: to dazzle audiences with their talents and stunt skills. They usually starts to train at an early age, shouldering the hopes and support of their families and teachers to become successful performers. We followed three of these young performers to show you how incredibly talented they are.

programme.title: Echoes of Green
programme.desc: Echoes of Green takes viewers deep into Anji’s bamboo forests and mountain villages, where a new generation of Chinese youth is redefining how to live with nature. Through stories of outdoor sports, forest cafés, tea rituals, and family farms, the series shows how ecological ideals have become everyday choices and creative lifestyles. It is not just about landscapes, but about people — young dreamers proving that prosperity and nature can grow side by side, and that culture gives life to sustainability.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the \"king of brocades.\" It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as \"weaving through the warp and cutting the weft\"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: A Vibe of the Village: The Beat of Xiangxi Drums
programme.desc: Experience the vibrant power of Xiangxi Drum Dance. This program delves into the heart of Tujia and Miao cultures, showcasing the rhythmic beats and spirited performances that have echoed through West Hunan's mountains for centuries. Discover the stories, traditions, and raw energy of this captivating folk art.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture.\nIn this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.

programme.title: Foodwise: Changsha
programme.desc: In this episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May embark on a journey to Changsha in Hunan Province.?They taste their way through the local cuisine, discover the city's latest art and fashion trends, and find out why this place is one of China's \"influencer hotspots.

programme.title: Foodwise：Qingdao
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Qingdao, China's beer mecca by the sea. With red tiles and green trees that make up the city's stunning scenery, our hosts embark on another foodie-journey with local delicacies and interesting guest appearances. From drinking beer with a German friend, catching crabs by the ocean and devouring grilled eel the classic Korean way, this episode will leave you hungry for more.

programme.title: Kung fu journey up Wudang Mountains
programme.desc: Located in central China's Hubei Province, Wudang Mountains is a UNESCO World Natural and Cultural Heritage site recognized as the cradle of Wudang kung fu and as a renowned Taoist sanctuary. Every year, young students from different parts of China flock to these well-established kung fu academies to experience a childhood like no other. Li Weimu and Li Muzi study at two separate kung fu academies, and are following their own paths at Wudang Mountains.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of \"World Capital of Stone Carving,\" awarded by the World Crafts Council.\nThe county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been influenced by the area’s close proximity to the sea.

programme.title: Huayin Old Tunes: the Earliest Rock and Roll by the Yellow River
programme.desc: Huayin Laoqiang is the name given to a traditional form of shadow puppet show. It translates as “Huayin Old Tunes,” and originated in the chanting of the boatmen working at the confluence of the Yellow, Weihe, and Luohe Rivers, close to the city of Huayin in Shaanxi Province. This chanting evolved into folk songs which, with time, were illustrated with puppet shows. The loud music, rustic instruments, and enthusiastic performances epitomize the way the local people lead their lives.

programme.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Colored stone inlay art: Transformation matters
programme.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident
programme.desc: Feicha, or a flying trident, is a type of performance where you balance a revolving trident on your body. In Chinese history, the trident was one of the 18 main weapons of war, but now it has become more of an acrobatic act. For thousands of years, people in China have practiced it as a performance and also as a way to keep fit.\nIt seems like the trident is stuck to the person’s body like a magnet, but that effect is achieved by tensing and relaxing muscles at the right time so that the movement of the trident is channeled in the right direction—be it around the hands, neck, or back. Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: Awakening Lion
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture.\nIn this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.

programme.title: Foodwise: Changsha
programme.desc: In this episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May embark on a journey to Changsha in Hunan Province.?They taste their way through the local cuisine, discover the city's latest art and fashion trends, and find out why this place is one of China's \"influencer hotspots.
